trajectory_summary: Helen was cooperative from first word to last — 'Okay. One sec.' at 02:30 confirms she was still following the rep's lead at the transcript's end. The call's energy leaked entirely from the rep side: no intro, no program pitch, mechanical verify, a fatigue self-disclosure, a diction miscommunication on the credit question, and a fragmented bill-collection instruction sequence. The call ends mid-process with Helen attempting to comply ('The letter. Copy letter.' at 02:32) but no appointment booked and no verify section completed. Outcome is attributable to rep-side execution gaps, not customer disqualification — no hard DQ signals were present.
outcome_dispute: v1 outcome tag is 'unknown.' Based on the transcript: appointment_booked=false. No DQ signals were present (homeowner confirmed, husband on title, credit confirmed over 670, bill in the $160-400 range). The incomplete outcome is rep-side execution failure, not customer disqualification. The call was not lost to objection or DQ — it simply ran out of coherent structure before reaching button-up.

primary_coaching_focus.topic: Pre-call state management — do not disclose impairment to prospects
primary_coaching_focus.quote: Yeah. Sorry. I just picked up. I'm I didn't get a lot of sleep last night, so I'm in a in a weird mode.
primary_coaching_focus.why: This is the call's root failure. Every downstream communication breakdown (garbled intro, credit question miscommunication, fragmented bill instructions) is consistent with a rep running in a degraded state. The disclosure itself is the symptom the manager needs to address first — not as a technique gap but as a floor standard: if you are not ready to run a call, you do not pick up the phone. Helen was cooperative throughout; this appointment was losable only from the rep side.

key_moments.quote: Hey. This is Bucky with ION reaching out about the solar for me filled online. How are you doing?
key_moments.stewart_read: Rep skips the Tesla/legitimacy framing and delivers a garbled standard intro ('solar for me filled online'). No program pitch follows at any point in the call. The schema's intros.standard template requires a reference to the form submission for legitimacy — the garbled delivery undermines that function. NOTE: coaching_philosophy.energy_match is marked PARTIAL/TBD in the schema pending Kenny's calibration; the pace and register assessment here is limited to what the transcript text supports.

key_moments.quote: Yeah. Sorry. I just picked up. I'm I didn't get a lot of sleep last night, so I'm in a in a weird mode.
key_moments.stewart_read: This is the call's single most damaging moment. The rep volunteers their own impairment to a prospect who had not noticed anything wrong. There is no rapport-repair function here — Helen had not complained. This is the rep managing their own discomfort at the customer's expense. NOTE: coaching_philosophy.energy_match is PARTIAL/TBD pending Kenny's calibration, but the principle that the rep's internal state should not be disclosed to the customer is not calibration-dependent.

Helen confirms her husband is on the title at 00:24. The rep acknowledges with 'Okay.' at 00:26 and never returns to the husband. The schema is explicit: co-title holders must be included in the appointment. The rep collected the information and did nothing with it — the protocol violation is not a missed question, it's a missed action.

Helen volunteers a bill range of $160-170/month (winter) and later references $190 and $300-400 (summer). The rep captures the numbers with 'Okay.' and moves on. The schema's bill_anchoring section is explicit: capture alone is necessary but not sufficient — the bill must be flipped into emotional alignment-against-the-utility. The rep had a $300-400 summer bill to work with and did nothing with it.
